static.description: Transform any legacy repository into an AI-agent-ready codebase by generating up to 12 scaffolding files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, system_prompt.md, agent-context.json, mcp.json, memory/schema.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, tools/refresh_context.py, .github/dependabot.yml, .github/CODEOWNERS, openapi.yaml, .agent-ready/custom_questions.json) using LLM-first analysis of actual code.
domain_concepts: agent-context.json: Machine-readable JSON file containing both static (user-editable) and dynamic (LLM-generated) metadata about a repository, used to ground AI agents

scaffolding files: The up-to-12 files AgentReady generates: AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, system_prompt.md, agent-context.json, mcp.json, memory/schema.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, tools/refresh_context.py, .github/dependabot.yml, .github/CODEOWNERS, openapi.yaml (REST APIs), .agent-ready/custom_questions.json
context drift: When the generated scaffolding files become stale relative to actual code changes, detected weekly by a drift detection workflow
readiness score: A 100-point score measuring how well-prepared a codebase is for AI agent interaction
provider preset: A named configuration mapping three pipeline phases (analysis, generation, evaluation) to specific LLM model strings
Phase 1 — Collect: Mechanical file reading phase that gathers file tree, source files, config files, CI config, and README without any LLM reasoning
Phase 2 — Analyse: LLM-powered phase that reads collected code and produces structured JSON understanding of the repository
Phase 3 — Generate: LLM-powered phase that writes each output scaffolding file from scratch based on the analysis JSON

Phase 5 — Evaluate: Assessment phase using a versioned golden question set (13 base + language overlay = 13–19 questions) with non-circular ground truth extracted from raw source code, a weak-model baseline (haiku-class), and hallucination detection. Measures whether context files genuinely improve AI responses.
restricted_write_paths: Paths that AI agents must never modify, identified during analysis and recorded in agent-context.json

MCP tool templates: Workflow YAML templates in src/agent_ready/templates/ used for installing trigger workflows into target repos. Do not confuse with the MCP server (mcp_server.py) which exposes transform/score/evaluate/review_pr as MCP tools.

dynamic.architecture_summary: AgentReady operates as a three-stage pipeline: (1) an analyser reads the target repository's source files and configuration to produce a structured JSON understanding, (2) a generator uses an LLM to produce scaffolding files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, agent-context.json, system_prompt.md, mcp.json, memory/schema.md) from that analysis, and (3) an evaluator runs golden-set questions against the repository with and without the generated context to measure improvement. The system is provider-agnostic via LiteLLM, supports seven LLM providers, and can be invoked via CLI, MCP server, or GitHub Actions workflows.

key_components.name: Analyser
key_components.responsibility: Phase 1+2: collects repository files mechanically and uses an LLM to produce a structured JSON analysis of the codebase.

key_components.name: Generator
key_components.responsibility: Phase 3: takes structured analysis JSON and uses an LLM to write each scaffolding file (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, agent-context.json, etc.) from scratch.

key_components.name: Evaluator
key_components.responsibility: Runs golden-set questions with and without context, uses a three-judge panel to score improvements, and produces AGENTIC_EVAL.md.

key_components.name: CLI
key_components.responsibility: Main entry point — parses arguments, resolves provider/model configuration, and orchestrates the analyse → generate → evaluate pipeline.

key_components.name: Ground Truth Extractor
key_components.responsibility: Extracts factual ground truth from raw source code via static parsing to eliminate circularity in evaluation.

key_components.name: PR Reviewer
key_components.responsibility: Reviews pull requests using LLM reasoning grounded in agent-context.json, producing structured APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES decisions.

key_components.name: MCP Server
key_components.responsibility: Exposes transform, score, evaluate, and review_pr as MCP tools for Claude Code and other MCP-compatible clients.

key_components.name: Golden Sets
key_components.responsibility: Versioned JSON question sets (base + language overlays) used by the evaluator for structured evaluation.

key_components.name: Run Transformer Script
key_components.responsibility: Convenience script that delegates to cli.main() for running the full transformation pipeline.

key_components.name: Changelog Generator
key_components.responsibility: Generates CHANGELOG entries from conventional commit messages since the last git tag.

agent_safe_operations: Adding new golden set JSON files in src/agent_ready/golden_sets/
Adding new test files in the tests/ directory
Modifying LLM prompts in analyser.py, generator.py, evaluator.py, and reviewer.py
Adding new provider presets in cli.py PROVIDERS dict
Updating version string in src/agent_ready/__init__.py and VERSION file together
Adding new MCP tools in mcp_server.py
agent_forbidden_operations: Removing or modifying the SKIP_AGENT_FILES / SKIP_AGENT_DIRS / SKIP_AGENT_WORKFLOWS filters in analyser.py — these prevent feedback loops
Feeding generated scaffolding files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, agent-context.json) back into the analyser as source code
Using shell=True in subprocess calls (security boundary enforced in reviewer.py)
Adding hard runtime dependencies — the project must remain zero-hard-deps (pure stdlib) for the core package
Modifying golden set base.json questions without updating the evaluator's expected question count
Interpolating user inputs directly into shell blocks (expression injection risk in workflows)
potential_pitfalls: The core package has zero hard dependencies (pure stdlib); litellm and mcp are optional extras under [ai]. Any code in analyser/generator/evaluator/reviewer must guard imports with try/except ImportError.

The analyser explicitly skips agent-generated files (SKIP_AGENT_FILES, SKIP_AGENT_DIRS, SKIP_AGENT_WORKFLOWS) to prevent feedback loops — modifying these filters can cause the LLM to treat scaffolding concepts as business domain.
Version is maintained in TWO places: src/agent_ready/__init__.py (__version__) and the VERSION file at repo root. Both must be updated together.
The evaluator expects a specific number of questions from golden sets; adding/removing questions in base.json or language overlays without updating the evaluator logic will cause assertion failures.
Ground truth extraction in ground_truth.py uses a minimal TOML parser (_toml_get) that handles only basic cases — complex TOML structures may not parse correctly.
LLM API calls in analyser.py, generator.py, evaluator.py, and reviewer.py all implement their own retry logic with exponential backoff — changes to retry behaviour must be applied consistently.
The reviewer.py enforces a trust boundary: PR diffs and descriptions are sent to the LLM but never executed locally. All subprocess calls must use argument lists, never shell=True.
Templates in src/agent_ready/templates/ are excluded from ruff linting (configured in pyproject.toml) — they contain YAML workflow files, not Python.
The setuptools config uses dynamic version from agent_ready.__version__; if the __init__.py import path changes, the build will fail.
Test coverage threshold is set to 50% (--cov-fail-under=50 in CI) — dropping below this will fail the CI pipeline.
